executive_summary: Business survived the full 2016-2025 window including the 2021-22 crisis. Net margin £1,243,337 (6.6% of revenue -- above 2-5% benchmark). Hedging cost £4,042,149 vs going naked (85% mandate price). 467 bill shock events (112 in crisis years); 17/18 retention offers accepted.

insights.headline: Hedging cost £4,042,149 vs going naked (85% mandate price)
insights.narrative: Whole-run commodity basis: actual net £1,243,337 vs naked £5,285,486. The 85% minimum hedge mandate cost £4,042,149 over the 10-year window. Worst year: 2022 (hedging cost £988,417 vs naked). Hedging reduces variance (survival objective) at the cost of expected P&L.

insights.headline: 467 bill shock events (112 in crisis years); 17/18 retention offers accepted
insights.narrative: Bill shocks across 2016-2025: 467 events, 9 extreme (>100%). 2021-22 accounted for 112 shocks as wholesale prices spiked. Retention: 17/18 offers accepted (94%); 5 customers churned without an offer (margin guard blocked). Enterprise value: £6,037,509 across 14 accounts.

insights.headline: Risk committee: 38 interventions; SURVIVED full 2016-2025 window
insights.narrative: The risk committee (Context Handshake) intervened 38 times over 10 years, with 9 in the 2021-22 crisis. Business survived: No administration event -- Ofgem solvency floor maintained.. The 85% minimum hedge mandate limited worst-case exposure.

insights.headline: Net margin £1,243,337 (6.6% of revenue -- above 2-5% benchmark)
insights.narrative: Gross margin £6,475,913, net £1,243,337 (6.6% of revenue). Industry benchmark: 2-5% -- this business sits above that range. I&C customers contribute 99% of net margin (£1,235,919); residential 1% (£7,418). WARNING: I&C accounts for 99% of margin -- high concentration risk. Cost to serve: £91,300; net after CTS: £6,370,846.

insights.headline: 1531 bills issued; service quality 0.905/1.0, clarity 0.816/1.0
insights.narrative: 1531 bills issued over 2016-2025 (avg 153/yr). Service quality: 0.905 (excellent). Bill clarity: 0.816 (good). Cost to serve: £91,300 portfolio total, £6,521/account/run.

coherence_narrative: The dominant theme is crisis survival. The business survived the full 2016-2025 window including the 2021-22 energy crisis, with the 85% hedge mandate cost £4,042,149 vs going naked, 112 bill shocks in the 2021-22 crisis, and 17/18 retention offers accepted. net margin £1,243,337 (6.5% of revenue -- above 2-5% benchmark), with I&C customers contributing 99% of net margin. The hedging mandate limited worst-case downside at the cost of expected P&L in calmer years; the I&C book was the primary profit engine throughout. Without I&C revenue the residential book would be loss-making -- concentration risk is the key strategic vulnerability.
recommended_actions: I&C concentration critical (99% of margin): target acquisition of 2 additional I&C customers to reduce dependency
Hedge mandate cost £4,042,149 vs going naked: review minimum hedge fraction for non-crisis years (current 85% floor)
112 crisis-year bill shocks: consider tariff smoothing mechanism to spread 2021-22 wholesale spike across 2020-2023 for residential customers
